issues.title: commands/view.md is out of sync with the new gogo-view behavior (reports-only description, no plans / :plan|:report grammar)
issues.description: skills/gogo-view/SKILL.md now enumerates a grouped Work/Changelog menu that includes plans, and adds the arg grammar `<slug>[:plan|:report]`. But the command wrapper commands/view.md was not updated: its `description` says "a gogo report", its `argument-hint` is "[changelog-entry | feature-slug]" (no `:plan`/`:report`, no mention of plans), and it embeds a stale 4-step flow that describes reports-only enumeration (`.gogo/changelog/*/ + .gogo/work/feature-*/report/`). This is the user-facing surface for FR1: a user sees an argument-hint that hides plan-viewing entirely, and the wrapper contradicts the skill it delegates to. It also re-introduces flow logic into a command (coding-rules: commands stay ultra-thin, no flow logic). The sibling commands/status.md WAS updated in this same stage (rewritten thin + delegating to the new gogo-status skill), so leaving commands/view.md stale reads as an oversight, not a deliberate FR5 deferral. Per code-review-standards this is an enumeration left out of sync (Major). Note: FR5 covers docs/* + README + version, which are correctly deferred; commands/view.md is the FR1 command surface, not a doc.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Update commands/view.md to match the skill: broaden the `description` to "a gogo plan or report"; change `argument-hint` to something like "[feature-slug[:plan|:report] | changelog-entry | path]"; and slim the body to a thin delegation (mirror the new commands/status.md pattern) that says: load `gogo-view` and follow it — enumerate grouped Work (each feature's plan + report) / Changelog (reports) newest-first, resolve an explicit arg per the skill's grammar (`<slug>` = report-else-plan, `<slug>:plan`, `<slug>:report`, `<date>-<slug>`, or a path), else present the AskUserQuestion grouped picker; build + open the page. Do not restate the enumeration logic in prose.
issues.fix_summary: Rewrote commands/view.md thin to match the gogo-view skill: description now covers a gogo plan OR report; argument-hint is "[feature-slug[:plan|:report] | changelog-entry | path]"; body delegates to gogo-view (grouped Work plans+reports / Changelog picker, arg grammar <slug>=report-else-plan / :plan / :report / <date>-<slug> / path, build+open best-effort) with no restated enumeration/flow logic (mirrors commands/status.md).

issues.title: skills/gogo/SKILL.md "View" section still describes reports-only enumeration (no plans, no grouped menu)
issues.description: The orchestrator skill's `### View -> command /gogo:view` section (skills/gogo/SKILL.md ~line 172) still reads "Read any report ..." and "Lists reports from .gogo/changelog/ and .gogo/work/*/report/" with no mention of plan bundles or the grouped Work/Changelog picker. coding-rules explicitly lists skills/gogo/SKILL.md as a mandatory enumeration-sync target (separate from README/docs, which are the FR5-deferred surfaces). After Stage A this blurb misdescribes the shipped capability. Lower impact than REV-001 (it is internal narrative, not the user-facing arg surface), hence Minor.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Update the View section to note that /gogo:view now surfaces both plans and reports via a grouped Work (each feature's plan + report) / Changelog (reports) picker, plans rendered in place from plan.md + charts/ (D1=A). One or two sentences; keep it terse.
issues.fix_summary: Updated the View section in skills/gogo/SKILL.md: it now reads "any plan or report", describes the grouped Work (each feature's plan + report) / Changelog (shipped reports) picker, and notes plans render in place from plan.md + charts/ (D1=A) — no longer reports-only.

issues.title: Generated plan page renders multi-line list items as broken lists (restarted numbering + stray leading-space paragraphs), undercutting FR3
issues.description:
In the evidence artifact .gogo/resources/view/viewer-bundles-and-done-board-plan.html, every soft-wrapped Markdown list item from plan.md is split during the summary->HTML pre-render: each list item becomes its own single-item `
    `/`
      ` (so ordered-list numbering resets to 1 on each item), and the wrapped continuation line becomes a separate `

      ` with the source's literal leading indentation/double-spaces preserved (e.g. "...ships exactly those..." and "...The commenter..."). This visibly degrades legibility on the flagship page for FR3, whose entire purpose is a readable article. Root cause: gogo-view Step 3 "Summary -> HTML" guidance maps `-`/`1.` to list items and blank-line-separated blocks to `

      `, but says nothing about coalescing soft-wrapped continuation lines that belong to the same list item/paragraph. The viewer.css typography itself is sound; the defect is in the pre-render step's handling.
issues.proposed_solution:
AGENT-FIXABLE. Add a line to gogo-view Step 3 "Summary -> HTML": before emitting, join soft-wrapped continuation lines (a non-blank line that continues the previous list item or paragraph, i.e. indented under a list marker or immediately following prose with no blank line) into that same `
  • `/`

    `, collapsing runs of whitespace to a single space; only a blank line starts a new block and only a line-leading marker starts a new list item (keep consecutive `1.`/`-` items inside one `

      `/`
        `). Then regenerate the sample plan page so the evidence reflects the fix.
issues.fix_summary:
Added explicit coalescing guidance to gogo-view Step 3 ("Summary -> HTML"): before emitting, join each soft-wrapped continuation line (a non-blank line that isn't a line-leading -/1./#/table/fence/blank) into the current
  • /

    with a single space; only a blank line starts a new block and only a line-leading marker starts a new list item, so consecutive 1./- stay in one

      /
        . Regenerated .gogo/resources/view/viewer-bundles-and-done-board-plan.html — ordered lists now render 4/3/7 items with no restarted numbering and no stray continuation

        (still offline, no http(s)://).

issues.title: Unplanned .gitignore change (ignore roadmap.md) is unrelated to Stage A
issues.description: .gitignore adds `roadmap.md` ("Internal roadmap - local backlog"). This is repo housekeeping with no tie to FR1/FR2/FR3 or the work-index classifier, and it is not in the plan's changes checklist. It brushes the NFR/coding-rules bar "don't auto-edit .gitignore; print guidance instead" (that bar targets gogo mutating a user project rather than gogo's own repo, so this is not a hard violation), but as an unrelated change riding along in the feature diff it hurts plan fidelity and diff hygiene. Low severity, non-blocking.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Either drop this hunk from the feature diff and land it as a separate housekeeping commit, or confirm it is intentional and note it in the plan/report so the change is accounted for. No functional change required.
issues.fix_summary: WONTFIX (intentional). The .gitignore roadmap.md entry is a deliberate change the user requested (gitignore the internal local-backlog roadmap); it is correct to keep and is not part of this feature. Left in place, unchanged.

issues.title: gogo-view SKILL.md H1 and opening line still say "reports" only after adding plan support
issues.description: The gogo-view skill's frontmatter description was updated for plans, but within the same file the H1 (line 16, "gogo-view - interactive viewer for gogo reports (FR8 / FR9 / FR10)") and the opening sentence (line 18, "Turns a report bundle (report.md + its .mmd diagrams) into one self-contained HTML page ...") still frame the skill as report-only. Minor in-file drift after a large edit; purely cosmetic but a fresh reader hits a contradiction between the intro and the new "Plans view in place too (D1=A)" paragraph two lines below.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Reword the H1 to "interactive viewer for gogo plans & reports" and generalize the opening sentence to "a plan or report bundle (plan.md / report.md + its diagrams)". Cosmetic only.
issues.fix_summary: Reworded the gogo-view skill H1 to "interactive viewer for gogo plans & reports" and generalized the opening sentence to "a plan or report bundle (plan.md / report.md + its .mmd diagrams)" — the intro no longer says reports-only, consistent with the "Plans view in place too (D1=A)" paragraph below.

issues.title: gogo-done TUI launch is fragile inside tmux / on a stale session, and a launch failure is silently treated as user-cancel instead of falling back to the table
issues.description: skills/gogo-done/SKILL.md "Board mode" Step 2 launches the kanban with `tmux new-session -s gogo-done "python3 ... board.py ..."` (lines ~167-168) and then, on exit, instructs (lines ~176-178): "Exit non-zero / no $res -> the user cancelled; ship nothing." This conflates two very different outcomes because board.py returns exit 1 both on a genuine user cancel (run_board returns 1 when the user presses q/ESC) AND on any crash/launch failure. Two concrete, verified launch failures are common: (1) when /gogo:done is run from inside an existing tmux session ($TMUX set) — the typical environment for tmux users — `tmux new-session` refuses to nest ("sessions should be nested with care, unset $TMUX to force") and returns non-zero WITHOUT ever running the board; (2) a leftover/duplicate session named `gogo-done` makes `tmux new-session -s gogo-done` fail ("duplicate session"). In both cases the board never renders, no $res is written, and the skill's rule interprets that as "user cancelled; ship nothing" — so the user is given neither the board nor the guaranteed status-table + AskUserQuestion fallback. That contradicts the skill's own Step 3 and Degradation sections ("no tmux / no python3 / no tty / **board error** -> the status table + AskUserQuestion multi-select ... never fail over the board") and the as-built sequence diagram (charts/done-board-flow.mmd line 17: "else no tmux / no python3 / no tty / board error" routes to the fallback), and it brushes the mandatory NFR portability/graceful-degradation bar. Note the dev host has no tmux, so this interactive path is essentially unexercised and the fragility went unnoticed; in practice the no-tty fallback (the common path, e.g. under Claude Code's non-tty Bash) is solid and fully specified. Non-blocking (it never hard-crashes /gogo:done and the far more common no-tmux/no-tty route degrades correctly), but it is a real robustness/UX gap in the interactive path for a scenario -- already being inside tmux -- that tmux users hit routinely.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. In gogo-done Step 2 make the launch nesting-safe and distinguish launch failure from cancel: (a) when `$TMUX` is set, use `tmux new-window` (or `tmux display-popup -E`, or prefix with `TMUX= tmux new-session`) instead of a bare `new-session`, and use a unique or `-A`/`kill-session`-guarded session name so a stale `gogo-done` session can't block the launch; (b) capture the tmux launch exit status separately from the board's own exit -- if the tmux command itself fails to start the pane (the board never ran), do NOT treat it as a cancel: fall through to the Step 3 status-table + AskUserQuestion fallback (which enforces the same ready-only guard). Only a clean board run that returns non-zero with no result file should be read as an explicit user cancel. This keeps the promise that any board error degrades to the table rather than silently shipping nothing.
issues.fix_summary: Made the gogo-done Board-mode TUI launch nesting-safe and split the three outcomes. Launch now branches on $TMUX: inside tmux -> `tmux new-window` (new-session refuses to nest) blocked via `tmux wait-for`; outside tmux -> `tmux new-session -A -s gogo-done-$$` (unique name so a stale/duplicate session can't collide). The board records its OWN exit code to `board-exit.code` (a tmux client's status is unreliable). Result handling now distinguishes: result file present -> confirmed, ship each slug; no result + code==1 -> the board ran and the user cancelled (stop, ship nothing); missing code / code==2 / any other value -> board error or launch failure -> the Step 3 status-table + AskUserQuestion fallback (never a silent no-op). Step 3 header + Degradation updated to route a tmux launch failure / exit 2 to the fallback; matches charts/done-board-flow.mmd. `git grep` shows no remaining bare new-session-only assumption.

issues.title: board.py raises an uncaught traceback on a missing or malformed --index instead of a clean error + distinct exit code
issues.description: assets/kanban/board.py load_index() opens the index path directly (line 49) and calls json.loads() on its contents (line 51) with no guard; both are reached from main() (line 328) before any curses setup. Verified by direct invocation: `--index does-not-exist.json` prints a full Python FileNotFoundError traceback and exits 1; a malformed index file prints a json.decoder.JSONDecodeError traceback and exits 1. This is defensive-only in the happy path (the gogo-done skill writes the work-index JSON itself with the Write tool immediately before launch, so it is well-formed), which is why it is low severity -- but two things make it worth tidying: a vendored user-facing tool dumping a stack trace is untidy, and (compounding REV-006) a non-zero exit from a load error is indistinguishable from a user cancel, so a malformed index would be silently read as "ship nothing" with no fallback. Empty/whitespace index is already handled correctly (returns [] -> an empty board), and the interactive no-tty case fails via curses with a non-zero exit and no result file, so no partial/garbage result is ever written.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. In board.py main(), wrap the `normalize_records(load_index(args.index))` call (or load_index itself) in a try/except for OSError and ValueError (json.JSONDecodeError subclasses ValueError); on failure print a one-line `board: cannot read work-index <path>: <reason>` to stderr and `return 2` (a distinct code the skill can tell apart from a cancel's 1). Optionally document the exit codes (0 ship / 1 cancel / 2 load-error) in the module docstring so gogo-done can branch on them per REV-006.
issues.fix_summary: Guarded board.py's index load: main() now wraps `normalize_records(load_index(args.index))` in try/except (OSError, ValueError) -- covering FileNotFoundError + json.JSONDecodeError -- printing a one-line `board: cannot read work-index <path>: <reason>` to stderr and `return 2` (a distinct code from a cancel's 1). Documented the exit-code contract in the module docstring (0=confirmed/result written, possibly {"ship":[]}; 1=cancel/no result; 2=error/bad-or-missing index or cannot start). Verified: missing --index, garbage --index, garbage stdin, and headless-with-garbage all exit 2 with one line and no traceback; empty/whitespace index still exits 0 (empty board); a normal --headless --ship a,b still emits {"ship":[...]} exit 0; py_compile + --selftest still green.
